title: Saying Goodbye on the Last Day before a Job Change
title_zh: 轉職前最後一天的道別

all.en: My last day at the company arrived faster than I had expected.
all.zh: 我在公司的最後一天比我預期得還快到來。

all.en: I packed my desk slowly, looking at each item and remembering its story.
all.zh: 我慢慢整理桌面，看著每樣物品，回想它背後的故事。

all.en: My favorite mug, a gift from my first team leader, went into the box first.
all.zh: 我最喜歡的馬克杯，是我第一位組長送的禮物，第一個被放進紙箱。

all.en: Colleagues stopped by all morning to wish me good luck and share memories.
all.zh: 整個上午都有同事過來祝我好運，並分享彼此的回憶。

all.en: We laughed about the project that almost gave us all a nervous breakdown.
all.zh: 我們笑著聊起那個差點讓大家神經崩潰的專案。

all.en: My closest workmate gave me a handwritten card that made my eyes water.
all.zh: 和我最親近的同事送了我一張手寫卡片，看得我眼眶泛淚。

all.en: At noon, the team took me out for a long farewell lunch.
all.zh: 中午時，團隊帶我去吃了一頓漫長的歡送午餐。

all.en: They presented me with a photo album filled with silly pictures from the office.
all.zh: 他們送我一本相簿，裡面滿是辦公室裡的搞笑照片。

all.en: I thanked everyone for shaping me into the professional I had become.
all.zh: 我感謝大家把我塑造成今天這個專業人士的模樣。

all.en: When I handed in my badge, the security guard wished me well like an old friend.
all.zh: 當我交回識別證時，警衛像老朋友一樣祝福我。

all.en: Walking out the front door, I felt both sad and ready for the next chapter.
all.zh: 走出大門時，我心中既有不捨，也準備好迎接下一個篇章。

all.en: That evening, I sat on my balcony and reviewed everything I had learned in those five years.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我坐在陽台上，回顧這五年來所學到的一切。

all.en: My phone buzzed with messages from former teammates wishing me a smooth transition.
all.zh: 我的手機收到前同事們的訊息，紛紛祝我順利過渡到新工作。

all.en: I opened the photo album again and laughed at a silly picture from our last office party.
all.zh: 我再次翻開相簿，看到上次辦公室聚會的搞笑照片就笑了出來。

all.en: The next morning, I had coffee with my mentor who had retired the previous year.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我與去年退休的導師一起喝咖啡。

all.en: He reminded me to keep learning and to never stop being curious about my work.
all.zh: 他提醒我要持續學習，不要對工作失去好奇心。

all.en: On the weekend, I organized my notes and prepared mentally for the new challenge ahead.
all.zh: 週末時，我整理筆記，並為即將到來的新挑戰做好心理準備。

all.en: I bought a new notebook and a smart blazer to start the fresh chapter properly.
all.zh: 我買了一本新筆記本和一件時尚西裝外套，認真展開新篇章。

all.en: My family threw a small dinner to celebrate this important career milestone.
all.zh: 家人辦了一場小型晚餐會，慶祝我這個重要的職涯里程碑。

all.en: They reminded me that change can be uncomfortable but often leads to real growth.
all.zh: 他們提醒我，改變雖然令人不安，但往往會帶來真正的成長。

all.en: On Sunday night, I set my alarm early, ready to walk into a brand-new office.
all.zh: 星期日晚上，我把鬧鐘設得很早，準備好走進嶄新的辦公室。

all.en: On my first day at the new company, my heart pounded as I rode the elevator up.
all.zh: 在新公司的第一天，搭電梯上樓時我的心臟怦怦跳。

all.en: The receptionist greeted me warmly and handed me a fresh badge with my name printed.
all.zh: 接待員親切地問候我，並遞給我一張印有我姓名的新識別證。

all.en: My new manager introduced me to the team during a relaxed welcome breakfast meeting.
all.zh: 我的新主管在一場輕鬆的歡迎早餐會議上把我介紹給團隊。

all.en: I noticed how different the office culture felt, with open desks and casual conversations.
all.zh: 我注意到這裡的辦公室文化很不一樣，採用開放式座位且氣氛輕鬆。

all.en: During lunch, two colleagues invited me to a nearby café to share their daily routines.
all.zh: 午餐時，兩位同事邀請我到附近咖啡館，分享他們的日常工作節奏。

all.en: By the end of the week, I had already learned everyone's name and a few inside jokes.
all.zh: 到了週末，我已經記住每個人的名字，也聽懂了幾個圈內笑話。

all.en: My first project was challenging, but my new teammates were generous with their support.
all.zh: 我的第一個專案具挑戰性，但新隊友們在支援上毫不吝嗇。

all.en: On Friday, I sent a brief update to my former mentor about how the transition was going.
all.zh: 星期五，我簡短地向以前的導師報告我目前轉換工作的進度。

all.en: He replied that growth often hides behind discomfort and encouraged me to keep going.
all.zh: 他回覆我說成長常常藏在不安之中，並鼓勵我繼續努力。

all.en: That weekend, I felt grateful for the past chapter and excited about everything ahead.
all.zh: 那個週末，我對過去的篇章心懷感激，也對未來的一切充滿期待。

all.en: During my first month at the new company, I attended several team-building workshops.
all.zh: 在新公司的第一個月，我參加了好幾場團隊建立的工作坊。

all.en: My new colleagues invited me to a karaoke night that helped break the ice quickly.
all.zh: 新同事邀請我去唱KTV，這場聚會很快地打破了彼此的隔閡。

all.en: I learned that asking thoughtful questions earned more respect than pretending to know everything.
all.zh: 我學到，提出有深度的問題比假裝什麼都懂更能贏得尊重。

all.en: By the end of my first quarter, I had successfully led my very first small project.
all.zh: 到了第一季結束時，我已經成功主導了我的第一個小型專案。

all.en: My former teammates organized a casual reunion dinner to hear about my new adventure.
all.zh: 我的前同事們安排了一場輕鬆的聚餐，想聽聽我的新冒險。

all.en: They were genuinely happy that my career move had brought such positive growth.
all.zh: 他們真心為我這次轉職帶來的正向成長感到開心。

all.en: I realized that maintaining old friendships took deliberate effort and consistent communication.
all.zh: 我意識到維繫舊友誼需要刻意的努力和持續的聯絡。

all.en: Every Sunday evening, I reviewed the week ahead and set three meaningful priorities.
all.zh: 每個星期天晚上，我都會檢視即將到來的一週，並設定三項有意義的優先事項。

all.en: My new manager invited me to mentor a junior colleague who had just joined the team.
all.zh: 我的新主管邀請我去指導一位剛加入團隊的新進同事。

all.en: Looking back, that brave career change became the best decision I had ever made.
all.zh: 回頭看，那次勇敢的職涯轉換成了我做過最棒的決定。

all.en: After six months at the new company, I received my first official performance bonus.
all.zh: 在新公司任職六個月後，我收到了第一筆正式的績效獎金。

all.en: I used part of that money to send a thank-you gift to my old mentor.
all.zh: 我用其中一部分錢，寄了一份感謝禮物給我以前的導師。

all.en: My junior colleague grew quickly under my patient guidance and weekly coaching sessions.
all.zh: 在我耐心指導與每週輔導之下，我帶的新進同事進步神速。

all.en: She thanked me publicly during the quarterly team meeting in front of senior managers.
all.zh: 她在季度團隊會議上當著高階主管的面公開向我致謝。

all.en: My new company offered me a small leadership role on a cross-department project.
all.zh: 新公司讓我在一個跨部門專案中擔任小型的領導角色。

all.en: I felt nervous but remembered the lessons my previous workplace had taught me.
all.zh: 我感到緊張，但回想起前一份工作教會我的所有經驗。

all.en: The project succeeded, and our results were shared across the entire company newsletter.
all.zh: 專案順利成功，我們的成果還被刊登在公司全體電子報上。

all.en: My former teammates sent congratulatory messages, proud to see my professional growth.
all.zh: 我以前的同事傳來祝賀訊息，為我看到專業成長感到驕傲。

all.en: On my one-year anniversary, I treated myself to a quiet weekend at a coastal hotel.
all.zh: 在我滿一週年的紀念日，我獨自到海邊飯店度過一個寧靜的週末。

all.en: Sitting by the ocean, I realized that brave decisions truly do open unexpected doors.
all.zh: 坐在海邊，我體悟到勇敢的決定確實能開啟意想不到的大門。

all.en: Three years into my new role, I received an unexpected invitation to give a keynote talk.
all.zh: 在新職位任職三年後，我意外收到擔任主題演講者的邀請。

all.en: I prepared my slides slowly, weaving in stories from both my old and current companies.
all.zh: 我慢慢準備投影片，將以前公司與現任公司的故事交織其中。

all.en: My old mentor flew into town just to sit in the front row and quietly encourage me.
all.zh: 我以前的導師特地飛來，只為了坐在前排靜靜地為我打氣。

all.en: When the audience applauded, I noticed my junior colleague crying happy tears in the back.
all.zh: 當聽眾鼓掌時，我注意到我帶過的新進同事在後排流下喜悅的淚水。

all.en: After the talk, several attendees asked whether I would consider writing a career memoir.
all.zh: 演講結束後，好幾位聽眾問我是否考慮撰寫一本職涯回憶錄。

all.en: I started drafting short chapters on weekends, using my old photo album for inspiration.
all.zh: 我開始在週末撰寫短篇章節，並以舊相簿作為靈感來源。

all.en: My partner read every draft and gently suggested adding more honest moments of doubt.
all.zh: 我的伴侶讀過每份初稿，溫柔建議加入更多誠實的懷疑時刻。

all.en: A small publisher agreed to print my book after reading only the first three chapters.
all.zh: 一家小型出版社讀完前三章後，就答應為我出版這本書。

all.en: On the launch day, my former team and my current team filled the bookstore together.
all.zh: 新書發表當天，我的前團隊與現任團隊一同把書店擠滿了。

all.en: Holding my book, I realized one brave career change had quietly authored an entire new life.
all.zh: 捧著我的書，我體悟到一次勇敢的轉職，悄悄寫下了一段全新的人生。

all.en: Readers from many cities wrote letters telling me how my honest stories had inspired them.
all.zh: 來自許多城市的讀者寫信告訴我，我誠實的故事如何啟發了他們。

all.en: One young woman said my memoir gave her courage to finally leave a job she had outgrown.
all.zh: 一位年輕女性說，我的回憶錄給了她勇氣，終於離開一份她早已超越的工作。

all.en: I began hosting small monthly workshops for professionals considering meaningful career changes.
all.zh: 我開始每月舉辦小型工作坊，給正在考慮做有意義職涯轉換的專業人士。

all.en: Each session ended with participants sharing one fear they wanted to release before moving on.
all.zh: 每場工作坊結束時，參與者都會分享一個他們想在前進前釋放的恐懼。

all.en: My current company supported these workshops by offering meeting rooms and free refreshments.
all.zh: 我目前的公司支持這些工作坊，提供會議室與免費的茶點。

all.en: On my fifth anniversary, the senior leadership invited me to join the executive advisory board.
all.zh: 在我滿五週年時，高階領導邀請我加入執行顧問委員會。

all.en: I accepted the role, promising to keep the same humble curiosity that had carried me forward.
all.zh: 我接下這個職位，承諾將保有一路支持我前行的那份謙遜好奇心。

all.en: Late one evening, I reread my old farewell card and smiled at how far the journey had stretched.
all.zh: 某個深夜，我重讀那張舊的歡送卡片，對這趟旅程延伸之遙微笑。

all.en: I tucked the card back into the photo album, ready to begin yet another exciting chapter.
all.zh: 我把卡片放回相簿，準備好展開另一個令人興奮的篇章。

all.en: Whatever came next, I knew that saying goodbye had taught me how to say a wonderful hello.
all.zh: 無論接下來會發生什麼，我知道告別已教會我如何說出美好的開場問候。

all.en: Years later, my advisory board work helped shape policies on flexible career transitions for younger employees.
all.zh: 多年後，我在顧問委員會的工作協助制定了關於年輕員工彈性職涯轉換的政策。

all.en: I often visited universities to speak with students about embracing uncertainty as a growth tool.
all.zh: 我常常造訪大學，與學生分享如何把不確定當作成長的工具。

all.en: One spring evening, I received an honorary award for contributions to workplace mentoring programs.
all.zh: 在一個春日的傍晚，我因對職場輔導計畫的貢獻而獲頒榮譽獎項。

all.en: My old farewell card still sat on my desk, its edges softened by years of careful handling.
all.zh: 我那張舊的歡送卡片仍放在桌上，邊緣因多年小心翻閱而變得柔軟。

all.en: Younger colleagues often asked how I stayed calm during big professional changes and uncertainties.
all.zh: 年輕同事常問我，如何能在重大職涯變動與不確定中保持冷靜。

all.en: I always answered that calmness grew slowly from honoring each goodbye with quiet gratitude.
all.zh: 我總是回答，冷靜是從以靜靜感激的心對待每一次告別中慢慢長出來的。

all.en: On weekends, I taught a free online course about navigating meaningful career chapters.
all.zh: 週末時，我開設一門免費線上課程，分享如何走過有意義的職涯篇章。

all.en: Hundreds of participants from many countries joined to share their hopes and quiet worries.
all.zh: 來自許多國家的數百位學員加入，分享他們的希望與內心的隱憂。

all.en: My partner often reminded me that being useful was its own kind of lasting career success.
all.zh: 我的伴侶常提醒我，能夠對他人有用，本身就是一種長久的職涯成功。

all.en: Each new dawn, I opened my journal and thanked that first brave farewell for everything afterward.
all.zh: 每個新的黎明，我都翻開日誌，感謝那第一次勇敢的告別所帶來的一切。

all.en: Last spring, my old security guard surprised me with a phone call after finding my number in a magazine article.
all.zh: 去年春天，那位老警衛在一篇雜誌文章裡找到我的電話，打了一通電話給我，讓我又驚又喜。

all.en: He had retired the previous year and now spent his afternoons walking his grandson to the neighborhood park.
all.zh: 他在前一年退休了，現在每天下午都會帶孫子去附近的公園散步。

all.en: We met for tea at a quiet café halfway between our two homes, and he brought me a small bag of homemade cookies.
all.zh: 我們約在兩家中途的一間安靜咖啡廳喝茶，他帶了一小袋自製餅乾給我。

all.en: He laughed remembering how nervous I had looked on my last day with that one cardboard box in my arms.
all.zh: 他笑著回憶我最後一天那副緊張的模樣，懷裡抱著一個紙箱。

all.en: I told him his quiet kindness back then had quietly carried me through my first lonely month of the new job.
all.zh: 我告訴他，他當年安靜的善意，悄悄陪我度過了新工作中孤單的第一個月。

all.en: Tears filled his eyes as he admitted he had always wondered whether the workers actually noticed his greetings.
all.zh: 他眼眶含淚，坦承自己一直在想，員工們是否真的有注意到他的問候。

all.en: We exchanged phone numbers and promised to meet for tea every other Saturday at the same little café.
all.zh: 我們交換了電話，約定每隔一個週六都到同一間小咖啡廳喝茶。

all.en: His grandson started joining us each visit and slowly learned to pour tea with both small careful hands.
all.zh: 他的孫子開始每次都加入，慢慢學會用兩隻小小心翼翼的手倒茶。

all.en: I gave the boy a copy of my memoir as a birthday gift when he turned ten the following autumn.
all.zh: 隔年秋天那男孩滿十歲時，我送了一本我的回憶錄當作生日禮物給他。

all.en: He hugged the book tightly, promising he would read it slowly when he was old enough to truly understand.
all.zh: 他緊緊抱著書，承諾等他長大到真正能讀懂時，會慢慢地閱讀它。

all.en: I cleared out my drawer and found notes I had forgotten about.
all.zh: 我清空抽屜時，發現了一些早已忘記的便條。

all.en: Saying goodbye to my desk felt stranger than I had imagined.
all.zh: 向我的座位道別，比我想像中更令人不捨。

all.en: My teammates surprised me with a card everyone had signed.
all.zh: 隊友們送我一張大家都簽了名的卡片，讓我驚喜。

all.en: I promised to keep in touch, and this time I really meant it.
all.zh: 我答應會保持聯絡，這一次我是真心的。

all.en: Walking out the front door one last time was bittersweet.
all.zh: 最後一次走出大門，心情百感交集。

all.en: Thank you all for teaching me so much over these years.
all.zh: 謝謝大家這些年來教會我這麼多。

all.en: We hugged at the elevator before I finally headed home.
all.zh: 在電梯口我們擁抱了一下，我才終於動身回家。

all.en: I left my plant for the new person who would take my seat.
all.zh: 我把盆栽留給將接手我位子的新同事。

all.en: My manager wished me luck and shook my hand warmly.
all.zh: 主管祝我好運，並溫暖地與我握手。

all.en: Endings are hard, but I knew a fresh start was waiting.
all.zh: 離別很難，但我知道一個全新的開始正在等著我。
